description_long: This is a small, twin-engine jet airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a low-wing configuration. The aircraft features swept-back wings with no visible winglets and a conventional T-tail design, where the horizontal stabilizer is mounted atop the vertical fin. The engines are mounted on the rear fuselage, one on each side, and the landing gear is of the tricycle type with two main wheels and a single nose wheel. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and is enclosed with multiple windows, providing visibility for the pilots. The exterior is painted in a gray camouflage pattern with military insignia and markings, suggesting a possible military or training role.
description: A compact, twin-engine jet airplane with swept low wings, T-tail, and rear-mounted engines, finished in gray camouflage with military markings.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the pointed nose, cockpit windows, swept low wings, and both rear-mounted engines clearly.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the T-tail, engine placement on the rear fuselage, and the left wing's roundel marking.
description_view_2: Rear-right view emphasizing the right wing, T-tail, and the symmetry of the engine mounts on both sides.
description_view_3: Front-right view displaying the cockpit, nose gear, and the right side of the fuselage with visible windows and markings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted on the rear fuselage, one on each side.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer mounted at the top.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear, all retractable.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
